action: Classify post-rename terminal-store failures as indeterminate and reconcile durable state before any retry.
finding: A directory-sync failure after atomic rename is an indeterminate commit outcome. Safe handling requires an immediate reread, exact-match acceptance, conflict rejection, and no blind retry when the write cannot be confirmed.
verification: Three deterministic Node tests passed in an isolated runtime using byte-identical candidate files.
falsification_route: Inject post-rename failures and fail the slice if the runtime retries before rereading, accepts a different terminal, or emits more than one compare-and-set attempt for an unresolved indeterminate outcome.
handoff: Wire one real peer-runtime caller to the filesystem store and propagate recorded-indeterminate-reconciled as a distinct evidence status. Then add crash injection around lock acquisition, rename, directory sync, and lock release.
